articles.title: Madhya Pradesh CM Mohan Yadav Launches UCC Suggestion Portal, Seeks Public Feedback Till June 15
articles.description: Madhya Pradesh has taken a major step towards implementing the Uniform Civil Code (UCC). Chief Minister Dr. Mohan Yadav launched the official consultation portal ucc.mp.gov.in and invited citizens, political parties and social organisations to share suggestions on the proposed law.The state government has set June 15 as the deadline for public feedback. Earlier, the government had formed a six-member committee headed by retired Supreme Court judge Justice Ranjana Prakash Desai to examine the feasibility of implementing the UCC in Madhya Pradesh.If implemented, Madhya Pradesh could become the fifth state in India to adopt the Uniform Civil Code.

articles.title: Union minister Harsh Malhotra appointed as BJP Delhi unit president | All you need to know about party’s new Delhi Chief

articles.title: BREAKING NEWS: Tension Erupts at Kalyan’s Durgadi Fort During Bakrid Prayers in Maharashtra
articles.description: A major controversy unfolded in Maharashtra’s Kalyan area during Bakrid celebrations after tensions flared at the historic Durgadi Fort, where members of Hindu organisations and Shiv Sena workers gathered during ongoing prayers. The situation led to heavy sloganeering and heightened security concerns as police closely monitored the sensitive location to prevent escalation. According to reports, the dispute is linked to a long-standing religious and legal conflict surrounding the Durgadi Fort premises in Thane district near Mumbai. The site has remained controversial for decades, with both Hindu and Muslim communities claiming historical and religious rights over the land. While one side considers the location associated with a temple dedicated to Goddess Durga, the other claims the presence of a mazar and links the site to Waqf property rights. During Bakrid prayers on Friday, groups associated with Hindu organisations reportedly assembled near the fort and conducted Hanuman Chalisa recitations, while raising slogans and reiterating their claim over the disputed site. Shiv Sena supporters carrying party symbols were also seen participating in the demonstrations. Local authorities deployed security personnel in the area as crowds gathered from both communities. Officials are said to be maintaining close surveillance to avoid any communal disturbance in the region. The dispute surrounding Durgadi Fort has a long political and social history dating back several decades. Political observers note that the issue has repeatedly resurfaced during religious occasions and festivals, often leading to heightened tensions and protests. The matter remains under judicial consideration, with court proceedings still pending regarding ownership and rights over the disputed premises. Meanwhile, authorities continue to appeal for peace and restraint as the sensitive issue once again draws attention across Maharashtra.

articles.title: CBSE digital evaluation row: Principals claim asked to defend system on social media

articles.title: POLITICAL ALERT: TMC Rift Deepens as Kakoli Ghosh Files Complaint Against Kalyan Banerjee
articles.description: Fresh internal tensions have surfaced within the Trinamool Congress after party MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar formally raised allegations of misconduct against senior TMC leader Kalyan Banerjee. The matter has now escalated beyond internal party discussions, with Kakoli reportedly approaching Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla seeking permission to file an official complaint against the senior parliamentarian. The political controversy comes at a sensitive time for the Trinamool Congress, which is already facing pressure following recent electoral setbacks and increasing signs of internal dissatisfaction within the party ranks. According to reports, Kakoli Ghosh had earlier resigned from key organisational responsibilities, including district-level positions, before expressing her concerns regarding remarks allegedly made by Kalyan Banerjee. In her communication, Kakoli reportedly objected to statements and behavior she considered disrespectful toward women. The dispute intensified after Kalyan Banerjee publicly responded to the allegations during a press conference, where he sharply criticized Kakoli and questioned her political stance after the party’s recent struggles. The public exchange has exposed growing divisions inside the TMC leadership, with political observers viewing the confrontation as a sign of deeper unrest within the organisation. The development has also sparked debate over internal discipline and leadership management inside the party. Sources suggest the issue may further escalate in Parliament if formal proceedings are initiated through the Speaker’s office. The controversy has generated major political attention in West Bengal, where opposition parties are also closely watching the developments. As tensions continue to rise, questions are being raised over whether the party leadership under Mamata Banerjee will intervene directly to contain the widening internal conflict before it impacts the party’s organisational structure and public image further.

articles.title: Hormuz standoff: Trump's 'will blow them up' warning to Oman amid fragile Iran-US peace talks
articles.description: Speaking to reporters during a cabinet meeting at the White House on Wednesday local time, Trump rejected the idea of any temporary arrangement that would allow Iran and Oman to jointly manage the strait. "No, the strait's going to be open to everybody," Trump said when asked whether he would support such a deal. Calling it an international passage, the US President stressed that no country would be allowed to dominate it.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Legendary German philosopher Friedrich Nietzsche: “I'm not upset that you lied to me, I'm upset that... – Inspiring lessons on relationships, honesty, understanding and why trust is built in years, broken in seconds by the writer known for his deep insights into human nature
articles.description: Quote of the Day by Friedrich Nietzsche: Friedrich Nietzsche’s quote, “I'm not upset that you lied to me, I'm upset that from now on I can't believe you,” explores the deep emotional impact of broken trust in human relationships. The quote suggests that the real pain of dishonesty is not the single act of lying, but the long-term damage it causes to faith, confidence, and emotional security between people.

articles.title: Greek Proverb of the Day: “A woman who cooks with a knife doesn’t joke” — Life lessons on strength, control, power, confidence, and why underestimating people can be your biggest mistake
articles.description: Greek Proverb of the Day highlights the meaning of “A woman who cooks with a knife doesn’t joke.” The saying reflects the idea that a woman who understands her strength, responsibilities, and control over her environment should not be underestimated or provoked. It suggests that true power is often expressed through confidence, skill, discipline, and calm authority rather than loud behavior or open display.

articles.title: Quote of the day by American philosopher Judith Butler: ‘The violence of language consists in its effort to capture the...’ – ‘Gender Trouble’ author on why words can never fully contain truth
articles.description: Judith Butler argues that language's attempt to fully define ineffable experiences, like love or grief, can be violent, destroying their essence. This quote highlights how words can limit reality, and Butler's work emphasizes that identity is fluid and shaped by social forces, not fixed definitions. Language should remain dynamic, allowing for ambiguity and growth.
